category_title: Probationary high school students with severe behavioral challenges and resistance to instruction
student_description: These are probationary students who exhibit extreme, sometimes dangerous disruptive behaviors (e.g., destroying property, attempting to set fires) when forced into traditional reading-based history instruction. They feel marginalized and hate school, but possess a strong affinity for art, requiring history to be taught entirely through visual research and artifact creation to maintain engagement and prevent severe behavioral outbursts.

criteria.rationale: Announcing that art will be part of the history lesson immediately captures the attention of students who otherwise hate the subject and feel like 'bad kids.'
criteria.check: The tutor's immediate response explicitly introduces an art, drawing, or visual creation element to the current task.

criteria.rationale: Forcing the student to read traditional text directly triggers extreme disruptive and dangerous behaviors.
criteria.check: The tutor does not demand, encourage, or redirect the student to read from a textbook or standard text passage.
